actionUnits.condition: Object has BoxCollider (enabled) and no Rigidbody. Attached MonoBehaviour (GUID: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is Fire.cs with serialized config: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. There are no UnityEvent m_Calls bound in this component. This trigger simulates a collider interaction to be paired with the external water system calling IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3). Expected external call: Fire.WaterHit(normal) -> sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval.

Precondition: Fire is currently ON (isOn==true). This represents a sustained water contact within maxHitInterval (2s), expecting Fire.Update() to accumulate currentHitTime toward totalHitTime (5s) while keeping currentHitInterval < 2s via repeated external WaterHit(normal) invocations.
Continue extinguishing: another water contact within 2s window to keep isTurningOff=true and accumulate currentHitTime (target: reach ~2s total).

Negative path: Pause water for > maxHitInterval (2s). Expected behavior in Fire.Update(): currentHitInterval >= maxHitInterval causes isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime reset to 0 (extinguish process canceled).
Restart extinguishing after reset: water contact within interval to set isTurningOff=true again (via external WaterHit).
Continued water contact within <2s to accumulate currentHitTime toward 5s.
Continued water contact; expected cumulative currentHitTime ~3s.
Continued water contact; expected cumulative currentHitTime ~4s.

Final water contact within 2s window to exceed totalHitTime (5s). Expected in Fire.Update(): isOn set to false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime reset, UpdateFireState(true) called (pSystem.Stop() and neighbor notifications via NotifyAllNeighbors; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1)).
